$schema_note: Format: { suppressions: [{ id, modules[], reason, expires(YYYY-MM-DD), references[]?, accept_reachable? }] }. Consumed by scripts/govulncheck.py. Each entry MUST document why the advisory is not exploitable in our usage and MUST set an expires date so suppressions are revisited. Reachable findings additionally require accept_reachable: true and a tracking issue in references.

suppressions.reason: The golang.org/x/crypto/openpgp package is deprecated and unmaintained by design (Go issue 44226); the advisory covers the whole golang.org/x/crypto module and has no fixed version (Fixed in: N/A). gokit uses golang.org/x/crypto only for maintained subpackages (ssh, bcrypt, etc.) and never imports any openpgp subpackage in any module (verified: no 'crypto/openpgp' import in the tree). govulncheck flags this at the require/import level across every module that transitively links x/crypto; the vulnerable package is not imported or called, so it is not reachable. Nothing to bump. Revisit if a maintained openpgp replacement becomes a hard requirement.
